content.problemStatement:
**This is the hard version of the problem. In this version, you must find the lexicographically maximum permutation that satisfies the conditions.

This problem is worth 404040 points in divisions 111 and 222, and is not scorable in divisions 333 and 444.**

You are given an integer NNN (N‚Č•3N\geq 3N‚Č•3).

Find a permutation‚Ä†^\dagger‚Ä† PPP of the integers 111 to NNN such that Pi‚Č†iP_i \neq iPi‚ÄčÓÄ†=i for every index iii, and

max‚Ā°i=1N(Pi‚äēi)\max_{i=1}^N \left( P_i \oplus i\right)i=1maxN‚Äč(Pi‚Äč‚äēi)is minimized.

Here, ‚äē\oplus‚äē denotes the bitwise XOR operation.

If there are multiple permutations that minimize this quantity, find the lexicographically maximum‚Ä°^\ddag‚Ä° one among them.

‚Ä†^\dagger‚Ä†A permutation of the integers 111 to NNN is an array of length NNN that contains every integer from 111 to NNN.

‚Ä°\ddag‚Ä°A sequence AAA of length NNN is lexicographically larger than another sequence BBB of length NNN if there exists an index iii such that Aj=BjA_j = B_jAj‚Äč=Bj‚Äč for every j<ij \lt ij<i, and Ai>BiA_i \gt B_iAi‚Äč>Bi‚Äč.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line NNN space-separated integers denoting the permutation you found.
constraints: The sum of NNN across all tests won't exceed 5‚čÖ1055\cdot 10^55‚čÖ105.
